failure_modes.text: False pattern recognition can force a threshold explanation onto a problem whose real structure does not match critical-mass dynamics.
failure_modes.mitigation: Test whether the problem actually has threshold dynamics before applying a critical-mass frame; not every growth curve has a tipping point.

failure_modes.text: Anchoring on one target threshold can understate how uncertain the path to durable mass really is.
failure_modes.mitigation: Size the uncertainty around the threshold: anchoring on one target number can understate how contingent and fragile the path to durable mass really is.

failure_modes.text: Strategic misrepresentation can make a team overstate current momentum or likely threshold reach just to secure resources.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit momentum claims against verifiable data; strategic misrepresentation about proximity to threshold is common when resources are at stake.

premortem_questions.text: Is this merely an analogy, or is the underlying structure truly the same?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is this merely an analogy, or is the underlying structure truly the same?

premortem_questions.text: What variables, beyond the threshold size, have we failed to account for?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What variables, beyond the threshold size, have we failed to account for, and what happens if the outcome is two standard deviations from our estimate?

premortem_questions.text: If this project fails, why did it happen?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If this project fails, why did it happen?

heuristics.text: Ask what density of the key resource is needed to reach the critical mass point in the relevant area.
heuristics.source_quote: What density of [resource] is needed to reach the critical mass point in a given area?

heuristics.text: Test the geographic or contextual boundary of the mass instead of assuming all local supply counts equally.
heuristics.source_quote: How far away can two tools be to count toward the same critical mass point in that area?

heuristics.text: If the threshold is unreachable as designed, change the model or seed the system so the threshold becomes reachable.
heuristics.source_quote: Can you tweak the business model so that this critical mass point is reachable or easier to reach?

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on threshold diagnosis, seeding logic, and false-threshold failure modes.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor marketplace taxonomy sprawl because the raw file is stronger on density, reachability, and threshold-fit checks.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses several startup-seeding examples into one reusable intervention move.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems thinking, feedback loops, momentum, and Pareto-style leverage for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is thinner than the structured tensions but clearly points to hammer effects and threshold overfitting.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against representativeness-bias, incentives, and confirmation-bias.
